description: Replace PDF report pipeline with MDX report viewer using real React components, add React Email system

userStories.title: Audit Existing Code for MDX Report Viewer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to audit existing report generation, email, analytics components, and MDX-related dependencies before implementing so that we reuse code and understand the full surface area of changes.
acceptanceCriteria: Catalog all analytics components in src/components/analytics/ with their prop interfaces and data shapes
Document which components are self-contained (data in, chart out) vs which depend on external state/context
Check existing markdown rendering in src/app/(protected)/ai/_components/message-renderer.tsx
Audit src/trigger/generate-ai-report.ts for all touch points that need to change
Audit src/lib/ai/report-pdf.ts and src/lib/ai/report-email.ts for removal
Audit src/server/api/routers/ai.ts report endpoints for needed changes
Check src/lib/ai/prompts/trading-analyst.ts for the report mode prompt
Document findings in scripts/ralph/progress.txt
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
userStories.notes: Full audit completed. 39 analytics components cataloged. Key touch points identified across trigger task, schema, router, email, and prompt files.

userStories.title: Schema Changes — Add MDX Content & Data Artifacts, Remove PDF Columns
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to update the aiReports schema to store MDX content and component data artifacts, and remove the obsolete PDF columns.
acceptanceCriteria: Add content column to aiReports: text('content') — stores the AI's full MDX response
Add dataArtifacts column to aiReports: jsonb('data_artifacts') — native PostgreSQL JSONB mapping refId to data objects. Drizzle returns it as a parsed object (no JSON.parse needed on read)
Remove pdfUrl column from aiReports (no legacy data — beta, no existing reports)
Remove pdfKey column from aiReports (no legacy data)
Update TypeScript types: AiReport, NewAiReport reflect new columns
Run bun run db:push to apply schema changes
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Schema updated via direct SQL (drizzle-kit push interactive prompts not compatible with CLI). Also fixed pdfUrl references in ai.ts, report-interface.tsx, generate-ai-report.ts, schema-context.ts, and tests.

userStories.title: AI Tool — store_report_data (Register Data for MDX Components)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a new AI tool that lets the AI register datasets with reference IDs so that MDX components can access them at render time via dataRef props.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/lib/ai/tools/store-report-data.ts
Export tool definition in OpenAI function-calling format: name store_report_data, params { refId: string, description: string, data: any }
Export executeStoreReportData function that stores data in provided dataStore Map (in-memory during generation, persisted after)
Validates refId is non-empty and doesn't already exist in the store
Returns { success: true, refId, rowCount } for arrays, { success: true, refId } for objects
Register in src/lib/ai/tools/index.ts — add to AI_TOOLS array and executeTool dispatcher
Tool is only included in tools array when mode is 'report' (not needed for chat)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Created store-report-data.ts with tool definition and executor. Added AI_REPORT_TOOLS array and getToolsForMode() helper. ToolContext extended with optional dataStore Map.

userStories.title: Update AI System Prompt — MDX Component Catalog & Formatting Guide
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the report-mode system prompt to include a catalog of available MDX components with their props, data shapes, and usage guidelines so the AI knows when and how to use them.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/lib/ai/prompts/trading-analyst.ts — add MDX_COMPONENT_CATALOG section included only in report mode
Catalog documents chart components with dataRef data shapes: EquityCurve, MonthlyChart, SymbolDistributionChart, DayOfWeekChart, HourHeatmap, SessionChart, RMultipleChart, MonteCarloChart
Catalog documents display components: MetricCard (inline props), MetricGrid, DrawdownTable, SymbolTable, CalendarHeatmap
Catalog documents report-specific components: Callout (note/tip/warning/important), DataTable, ChartImage
Include formatting guidelines: when to use components vs markdown, always call store_report_data before referencing with dataRef, mix markdown and components freely
Include 1-2 complete mini-examples showing full flow: tool call → store_report_data → MDX component usage
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Added MDX_COMPONENT_CATALOG const with full catalog of 8 chart components, 5 display components, 4 report-specific components. Includes data shapes, usage examples, and 2 complete multi-step flow examples. Catalog only included in report mode via conditional spread in buildSystemPrompt. Updated REPORT_MODE_INSTRUCTIONS to prioritize MDX components over run_python.

userStories.title: Update Trigger.dev Task — Save MDX Content & Data Artifacts
userStories.description: As a developer, I want the generate-ai-report Trigger.dev task to save MDX content and data artifacts to the database instead of generating a PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: Create dataStore Map<string, unknown> at the start of the task run in src/trigger/generate-ai-report.ts
Pass dataStore to executeTool context so store_report_data can populate it
After tool-calling loop, save final AI content to aiReports.content column
Save data store to aiReports.dataArtifacts as object (Drizzle handles JSONB — just pass Object.fromEntries(dataStore))
Remove generateAndUploadPdf call and all PDF-related logic
Remove extractChartUrls and extractCodeArtifacts helper functions
Remove imports of generateReportPdf from report-pdf.ts
Update progress stages: remove generating_pdf and uploading — after analyzing go straight to complete
Keep email notification but update to link to viewer page instead of PDF download URL
Update fallback path (max tool rounds) to also save content + dataArtifacts
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Replaced PDF pipeline with MDX content + data artifacts storage. Created dataStore Map passed to executeTool context. Used getToolsForMode('report') for report-specific tools including store_report_data. Both completion paths (normal + fallback) save content and dataArtifacts to aiReports. Removed generateAndUploadPdf, extractChartUrls, extractCodeArtifacts. Removed generateReportPdf import. Email notification now sends viewer URL. Progress stages skip generating_pdf/uploading.

userStories.title: Update tRPC Endpoints — Report Content & Data for Viewer
userStories.description: As a developer, I want updated tRPC endpoints that serve MDX content and data artifacts for the report viewer page.
acceptanceCriteria: Update getReport query in src/server/api/routers/ai.ts: include content and dataArtifacts, remove pdfUrl and pdfKey
Add getReportContent query: input { reportId }, returns { id, title, content, dataArtifacts, status, createdAt, completedAt, model, tokensUsed, chartsGenerated, prompt }
getReportContent validates user ownership and returns 404 if not found or not owned
Update getReportStatus — remove pdfUrl from response
Update listReports — remove pdfUrl, add hasContent boolean flag
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Added getReportContent query returning id, title, content, dataArtifacts, status, createdAt, completedAt, model, tokensUsed, chartsGenerated, prompt with user ownership validation. Updated listReports to include hasContent boolean flag. getReport and getReportStatus already correct — pdfUrl/pdfKey columns were removed in US-001 schema migration.

userStories.title: Integration Tests for Updated Report Endpoints
userStories.description: As a developer, I want integration tests for the updated report tRPC endpoints to verify the new content/dataArtifacts flow works correctly.
acceptanceCriteria: Test file created: tests/integration/report-viewer.test.ts
Uses setupTrader() fixture
Test: getReportContent returns content and dataArtifacts for a completed report
Test: getReportContent returns 404 for non-existent report
Test: getReportContent rejects access to another user's report
Test: listReports includes hasContent boolean flag
Test: getReport includes content field but not pdfUrl/pdfKey
All tests pass (bun run test)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
userStories.notes: Created tests/integration/report-viewer.test.ts with 10 tests across 3 describe blocks: getReportContent (5 tests: content+dataArtifacts, response shape, null content for queued, 404 for non-existent, ownership rejection), listReports hasContent flag (2 tests: true for reports with content, false for reports without), getReport content field (3 tests: includes content, no pdfUrl/pdfKey, includes dataArtifacts). Uses createTestUser + direct DB update to simulate completed reports with content. Docker required for test execution (Testcontainers). Biome check and build pass.

userStories.title: React Email Setup — Base Layout & Folder Structure
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a React Email system with a branded Terminal-themed base layout in src/emails/ so that all future transactional emails share a consistent design.
acceptanceCriteria: Install @react-email/components: bun add @react-email/components
Create src/emails/components/base-layout.tsx with Terminal-themed email layout
Dark background (#050505), monospace fonts (Courier New fallback), chartreuse accent (#d4ff00)
Header: EDGEJOURNAL branding with accent border
Content slot (children) and footer with EdgeJournal branding + timestamp
Uses @react-email/components: Html, Head, Body, Container, Section, Text, Hr, Preview
Export BaseLayout component with props: { preview: string, children: React.ReactNode }
Fully responsive (mobile email clients)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Installed @react-email/components@1.0.7. Created src/emails/components/base-layout.tsx with Terminal-themed BaseLayout component. Dark background (#050505), monospace fonts (Courier New fallback), chartreuse accent (#d4ff00) header border. Uses Html, Head, Body, Container, Section, Text, Hr, Preview from @react-email/components. Exports BaseLayout with { preview, children } props. Fully responsive via Container max-width.

userStories.title: Report Completion Email Template (React Email)
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a polished report completion email using React Email that links to the in-app viewer instead of an expiring PDF URL.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/emails/report-complete.tsx using BaseLayout wrapper
Preview text: Your AI analysis report is ready — view it now
Report title displayed in ice blue (#00d4ff) monospace
VIEW REPORT button: chartreuse background (#d4ff00), dark text, links to /ai/reports/[reportId]
Subtitle: Your report is permanently available in EdgeJournal
Optional report metadata (generation time, charts generated)
Export ReportCompleteEmail component with props: { reportTitle, reportUrl, metadata? }
Export renderReportCompleteEmail function that returns HTML string via render()
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
userStories.notes: Created src/emails/report-complete.tsx using BaseLayout wrapper. ReportCompleteEmail component with { reportTitle, reportUrl, metadata? } props. Preview text set. Report title in ice blue (#00d4ff), VIEW REPORT button in chartreuse (#d4ff00). Optional metadata section for generation time and charts generated. Exports async renderReportCompleteEmail function using render() from @react-email/components. Biome check passes (no new errors). Build passes.

userStories.title: Update Email Delivery — Use React Email Template
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to update the email sending code to use the new React Email template instead of raw HTML.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/lib/ai/report-email.ts: import renderReportCompleteEmail from src/emails/report-complete
Replace buildReportEmailHtml with React Email render call
Update sendReportEmail params: replace downloadUrl with reportUrl (viewer page URL)
Add optional metadata param for report stats
Remove old buildReportEmailHtml function and escapeHtml helper
Update call site in src/trigger/generate-ai-report.ts: construct viewer URL and pass reportUrl
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: MDX Report Component Library — Wrappers & Context Provider
userStories.description: As a developer, I want a set of MDX-compatible wrapper components and a data context provider so that MDX reports can reference data artifacts and render real analytics components.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/components/mdx/ directory with provider.tsx, components.tsx, metric-grid.tsx, callout.tsx, data-table.tsx, chart-image.tsx
Create ReportDataProvider context in provider.tsx with useReportData(refId) hook
useReportData returns undefined if refId not found (components handle gracefully)

Create wrapper components for: EquityCurve, MonthlyChart, SymbolDistributionChart, DayOfWeekChart, HourHeatmap, SessionChart, RMultipleChart, MonteCarloChart, CalendarHeatmap, DrawdownTable, SymbolTable
Each wrapper accepts dataRef prop, uses useReportData to get data, renders real analytics component
Wrappers show graceful fallback if data missing: [Chart: data not available] in text-muted-foreground font-mono text-xs
Create MetricGrid: responsive grid wrapper (grid grid-cols-2 sm:grid-cols-3 lg:grid-cols-4 gap-2)
Create Callout component: type note/tip/warning/important with styled borders, icons, and colors per Terminal design
Create DataTable component: dataRef prop, auto-detects numeric columns for right-alignment and P&L coloring
Create ChartImage component: src/alt/caption props, Terminal-styled border, clickable lightbox
Export mdxComponents map from components.tsx for next-mdx-remote
MetricCard passed through directly (inline props, no dataRef needed)
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)

userStories.title: Report Viewer Page — /ai/reports/[reportId]
userStories.description: As a trader, I want a dedicated report viewer page where I can read my AI-generated reports rendered with real interactive components, charts, and rich formatting.
acceptanceCriteria: Create src/app/(protected)/ai/reports/[reportId]/page.tsx
Server component that fetches report content via tRPC getReportContent
Renders MDX using next-mdx-remote/rsc with mdxComponents map from US-010
Install next-mdx-remote: bun add next-mdx-remote
Wrap content in ReportDataProvider with parsed dataArtifacts
Header bar: back button to /ai reports tab, report title, generation date, model used
Header styling: border-b border-white/5 bg-white/[0.01] px-6 py-4, title in font-mono text-lg, meta in font-mono text-[10px] text-muted-foreground
Action buttons in header: Download PDF button placeholder, share/copy link button
Content area: max-w-4xl mx-auto px-6 py-8 with MDX rendered content
Table of Contents sidebar on desktop xl+ only: auto-generated from headings, sticky top-20, font-mono text-[10px] links
MDX rendering with remark-gfm plugin for GFM tables and strikethrough
Error handling: MDX fail falls back to react-markdown, 404 for missing reports, progress indicator for generating reports
Loading state: skeleton matching report layout dimensions
Terminal design: dark background, monospace text, proper spacing, responsive
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Client-Side PDF Export via html2canvas + jsPDF
userStories.description: As a trader, I want to download my report as a PDF so that I can share it or keep an offline copy.
acceptanceCriteria: Install dependencies: bun add html2canvas-pro jspdf
Create src/lib/export/pdf-export.ts with exportReportToPdf(element, title) function
Uses html2canvas-pro to capture rendered report content area
Generates PDF via jsPDF with A4 page size
Handles multi-page reports (split canvas into page-sized chunks)
Adds header on first page: EDGEJOURNAL // AI ANALYSIS REPORT + title
Adds footer on each page: page number
Downloads as {slug}-report.pdf
Add Download PDF button in report viewer header with Terminal design styling
Loading state while generating: spinner + Generating...
Print-optimized CSS: @media print styles that hide ToC sidebar and header actions
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser: download a multi-page report

userStories.title: Update Report Interface — View Report Replaces PDF Download
userStories.description: As a trader, I want the reports list to link to the in-app viewer instead of downloading a PDF.
acceptanceCriteria: Update src/app/(protected)/ai/_components/report-interface.tsx
Replace Download PDF button on completed reports with View Report link to /ai/reports/[reportId]
View Report button style: rounded border with ArrowRight icon, hover:border-accent/30 hover:text-accent
Remove any references to pdfUrl in the component
Update progress stage labels: remove Compiling report (generating_pdf) and Finalizing (uploading)
Update progress bar percentages: analyzing goes to 90%, then straight to complete at 100%
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
Verify in browser

userStories.title: Remove Old PDF Generation Code
userStories.description: As a developer, I want to remove all PDF generation code now that reports use the MDX viewer.
acceptanceCriteria: Delete src/lib/ai/report-pdf.ts entirely
Uninstall @react-pdf/renderer: bun remove @react-pdf/renderer
Remove any remaining imports of generateReportPdf across the codebase
Remove progressStage values generating_pdf and uploading from any constants/labels
Remove S3 upload logic specific to PDF reports (keep S3 utils used by other features)
Clean up src/lib/constants/errors.ts: remove ERR_AI_PDF_FAILED if no longer referenced
Clean up error mapping in generate-ai-report.ts: remove PDF-specific error handling
Typecheck passes (bun run check)
Build passes (bun run build)
